toolCalls.name: write_document
toolCalls.arguments: {"document":"A regular computer stores information in bits — tiny switches that are either on (1) or off (0). A quantum computer uses qubits, which can sit in a fuzzy superposition of both states at once until you check them. Stack many qubits together and they can explore lots of possibilities in parallel, which is why people are excited.\n\nThis doesn't make quantum computers faster at everything. They're great at problems with hidden structure — like factoring big numbers, simulating molecules, or searching certain databases — but useless for, say, opening Excel. Today's machines are noisy and small, so we mostly use them to test ideas rather than replace your laptop."}
